assertions.title: Docs-only governance is narrow and consistent
assertions.reason: The mission guidance no longer contradicts the executable policy: repo-root and canonical governance docs still define the same narrow docs-only allowlist, and the script routes that allowlist to `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` only while skipping Codex review/subagent/runtime validators.

steps.expected: Mission guidance keeps the lint-only exception and does not claim docs-only work skips validators entirely.
steps.observed: `mission.md` now says docs-only packets still run `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh` as the sole docs-only validator while skipping scrutiny and runtime/user-testing validators.

steps.action: Inspect repo-root and canonical governance docs (`AGENTS.md`, `docs/CONVENTIONS.md`, `docs/agents/PERMISSIONS.md`, `docs/agents/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/SECURITY.md`).
steps.expected: The same narrow docs/governance lane and the same skip-review/runtime expectations are documented consistently.
steps.observed: Those governance files all restate the canonical docs/governance allowlist and the same `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh`-only validation behavior for docs-only packets.

steps.expected: The executable classifier uses the same allowlist and routes only that lane through knowledgebase lint while sending everything else to strict review checks.
steps.observed: The script whitelist matches the documented lane and emits `docs-only change-set detected` plus `run knowledgebase lint only and skip Codex review/subagent/runtime validators`; non-allowlisted paths fall back to strict review checks.

steps.action: Compare mission, root, canonical-doc, and script policy surfaces.
steps.expected: No remaining contradiction about docs-only scope or validator expectations.
steps.observed: The prior contradiction is resolved; all inspected policy surfaces now align on the narrow docs-only lane and lint-only validation path.

summary: Re-tested 1 assertion: 1 passed. VAL-DOCS-006 now passes because mission guidance, repo-root/canonical governance docs, and the executable review-policy script all agree on the same narrow docs-only lane and the same `bash ci/lint-knowledgebase.sh`-only validation behavior.
